purpose: Offline exercise library for shoulder and arm rehabilitation planning
library_note: The planner catalogue is broader than the motion-analysis demo. Only the pendulum exercise is tracker-ready in the current repository; other exercises remain planner-available and can be connected later to additional kinematic modules.

pain_monitoring.next_morning_rule: Pain should settle by the following morning and should not increase week to week

progression_rules.high_irritability: Use gentle mobility, low load, low volume, daily frequency if tolerated
progression_rules.moderate_irritability: Use controlled mobility and light strengthening
progression_rules.low_irritability: Use progressive strengthening and functional exercise

exercises.name: Pendulum Exercise
exercises.instructions: Lean forward slightly with the unaffected arm supported on a table. Let the affected arm hang relaxed. Gently move the arm in small circles or forward and backward. Keep the shoulder relaxed and avoid forcing the movement.
exercises.avoid: Do not swing aggressively or push into sharp pain.
exercises.xai_reason: Selected because early-stage or irritable shoulder pain benefits from gentle movement with low load.

exercises.name: Scapular Setting
exercises.instructions: Sit or stand tall. Gently draw the shoulder blades back and slightly down as if setting the shoulder into a stable position. Hold briefly, then relax.
exercises.avoid: Do not shrug the shoulders or arch the back.
exercises.xai_reason: Selected because scapular control supports shoulder movement and can be used at low intensity.

exercises.name: Wall Slide
exercises.instructions: Stand facing a wall. Place the hand of the affected arm on the wall and slowly slide it upward as far as comfortable. Pause briefly, then slide back down.
exercises.avoid: Do not force overhead range or continue if pain becomes sharp.
exercises.xai_reason: Selected because it improves shoulder range of motion in a controlled and supported way.

exercises.name: External Rotation with Resistance Band
exercises.instructions: Stand with the elbow bent to 90 degrees and held close to the side. Hold a light resistance band and rotate the forearm outward slowly, then return with control.
exercises.avoid: Do not let the elbow move away from the body or use heavy resistance too early.
exercises.xai_reason: Selected because rotator cuff strengthening is commonly used for shoulder control and function.

exercises.name: Internal Rotation with Resistance Band
exercises.instructions: Stand with the elbow bent to 90 degrees and close to the side. Pull the resistance band inward across the body slowly, then return with control.
exercises.avoid: Do not twist the trunk or pull through pain.
exercises.xai_reason: Selected because it strengthens shoulder internal rotators while maintaining controlled movement.

exercises.name: Isometric External Rotation
exercises.instructions: Stand next to a wall with the elbow bent to 90 degrees. Gently press the back of the hand outward into the wall without moving the arm. Hold, then relax.
exercises.avoid: Do not press hard enough to increase symptoms above the pain limit.
exercises.xai_reason: Selected because isometric loading can begin strengthening with minimal joint movement.

exercises.name: Doorway Chest Stretch
exercises.instructions: Place the forearm on a doorway with the elbow below or around shoulder height. Step forward gently until a stretch is felt across the chest. Hold without bouncing.
exercises.avoid: Do not overstretch or place the shoulder in a painful position.
exercises.xai_reason: Selected because chest flexibility may support shoulder positioning and movement.

exercises.name: Biceps Isometric Hold
exercises.instructions: Bend the elbow to about 90 degrees. Place the other hand on top of the forearm and gently push upward against resistance without moving the elbow. Hold, then relax.
exercises.avoid: Do not hold your breath or push into sharp pain.
exercises.xai_reason: Selected because controlled isometric loading may be used when tendon symptoms are irritable.

exercises.name: Wrist Extensor Eccentric Exercise
exercises.instructions: Rest the forearm on a table with the wrist over the edge and palm facing down. Use the other hand to help lift the wrist up, then slowly lower it down under control.
exercises.avoid: Do not use heavy weight early or continue if pain exceeds the safe pain threshold.
exercises.xai_reason: Selected because progressive tendon loading is commonly used for lateral elbow tendon pain.

exercises.name: Wrist Flexor Eccentric Exercise
exercises.instructions: Rest the forearm on a table with the wrist over the edge and palm facing up. Use the other hand to help lift the wrist, then slowly lower it under control.
exercises.avoid: Do not grip too tightly or load through sharp pain.
exercises.xai_reason: Selected because progressive tendon loading is commonly used for medial elbow tendon pain.
